task: Read notes.txt and write a file todo2.txt that contains ONLY the 2nd TODO line (exactly, including the 'TODO:' prefix). Then call final with a 1-line confirmation.
Count how many lines are in alpha.txt and beta.txt (use tools, do not guess). Write a JSON file counts.json with keys alpha_lines and beta_lines as integers. Then final with the JSON on one line.
Write a Python script fib.py that prints the first 12 Fibonacci numbers (one per line). Run it with python3 fib.py and write the output to fib.txt. Then final with 'OK'.

Create a file data.csv with header 'name,score' and 5 rows. Then write a Python script stats.py that reads data.csv and prints the average score as a number with 2 decimals. Run it and write the printed line to avg.txt. Then final with the average.
Fetch https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved . Extract the page title and write it to iana_title.txt (single line). Then final with the title.
Fetch https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc2606 . Write rfc2606.md with: (1) a 2-sentence summary, (2) 3 bullet key points, (3) the source URL. Then final with 'written rfc2606.md'.
Fetch https://example.com and https://example.org . Write compare.md with: (a) one-line description of each page, (b) 2 differences, (c) both URLs. Then final with 'compare.md done'.

Fetch https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8259 . Identify the official title of the document and write it into title.txt. Then write a Python script check_title.py that reads title.txt and prints the number of characters in the title. Run it and write the output to title_len.txt. Then final with the character count.
Read notes.txt. Create a new file notes_clean.md that contains ONLY the TODO lines as a markdown checklist. Then final with the number of TODO items.
Write a Python script grep_notes.py that reads notes.txt and prints only lines containing 'TODO'. Run it and save output to todos.txt. Then final with 'todos saved'.
Read alpha.txt, sort lines alphabetically, write to alpha_sorted.txt (same lines, one per line). Then final with 'alpha_sorted.txt written'.
Write a Python script unique_words.py that reads alpha.txt and prints the unique words in sorted order, one per line. Run it and save output to alpha_unique.txt. Then final with 'done'.
Create merged.txt by concatenating alpha.txt then beta.txt (exact order, keep original line breaks). Then final with the number of lines in merged.txt.
Read notes.txt and write todos.json as a JSON array of strings containing ONLY the TODO lines (exact lines). Then final with the array length.
Write primes.py that prints all prime numbers <= 50 (one per line). Run it and save to primes.txt. Then final with 'OK'.

Create words.txt with 8 words, including at least 2 palindromes. Write palindrome.py that prints only palindromes from words.txt. Run it and save to palindromes.txt. Then final with how many palindromes found.

Write a JSON file person.json with keys name (string) and age (integer). Then write validate.py that reads person.json and prints 'valid' if age is an integer >=0 else 'invalid'. Run it and write output to validate.txt. Then final with the printed word.
Fetch https://example.com and write a file example_status.txt containing ONLY the HTTP status code as digits. Then final with the same digits.
Fetch https://example.com . Extract the first <h1> text and write to example_h1.txt (single line). Then final with that line.

Fetch https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved . Write reserved_domains.md listing the reserved domains mentioned (as bullet list). Include the source URL. Then final with how many bullet items you listed.
Fetch https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc2119 . Write rfc2119.md containing: title line, 3 bullet points of key ideas, and the source URL. Then final with 'rfc2119.md written'.

Fetch https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved . Extract all reserved domain names you can find. Write them to domains.txt (one per line). Then write count_domains.py that counts lines in domains.txt and prints the count. Run it and write output to domain_count.txt. Then final with the count.
Write report.md with 3 sections: Intro, Data, Conclusion. In Data, include a 2-column markdown table with 3 rows of sample numbers. Then final with 'report.md done'.
Write nums.txt with 6 integers (one per line). Write sum.py that reads nums.txt and prints their sum. Run it and save output to sum.txt. Then final with the sum.
Fetch https://www.iana.org/ . Extract the page title and write it to iana_home_title.txt. Then final with the title.
Read notes.txt, count how many lines start with TODO and how many start with DONE. Write counts.md with both counts. Then final with both counts in one line like 'TODO=X DONE=Y'.
Create a markdown file template.md with a title, 3 bullet points, and a source line 'Source: internal'. Then final with 'template.md created'.
Read beta.txt and write beta_first3.txt containing ONLY the first 3 lines. Then final with the last line of beta_first3.txt.
Write nums.txt with 11 integers (one per line). Write sum.py that reads nums.txt and prints their sum. Run it and save output to sum.txt. Then final with the sum.
